novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The comparator_promise_table claims strict_tightening or iff_frontier against Aronow-Samii, Savje, Auerbach-Guo-Tabord-Meehan, and Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang, but no Section 8 headline is phrased as a strict tightening or iff frontier against any named published result.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang testing-impossibility comparator is cited only at paper level for the flagship gap; no section, theorem, equation, or quoted open-problem location anchors the claimed finite frontier.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The conjecture quantifies over every two-regular graph, but Assumption 1 fixes only the two candidate graphs C6 and 2K3.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: After unfolding the local quadratic response, mu_G depends on G only through the already-defined average closure count tau_cl; the proposed frontier is essentially linearity plus the definition of the quotient, below the three-step nontriviality floor.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the two-regular closure specialization with a genuine comparator-anchored theorem: name the exact theorem or open problem in Auerbach-Guo-Tabord-Meehan or Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang and state a strict iff frontier against it.
Generalize the graph class beyond two-regular aliases and give an explicit decision procedure for the minimal exposure invariant, with input format, predicate, and complexity or termination bound.
Patch the current statement by adding assumptions for a general finite two-regular graph class instead of reusing the C6/2K3 graph-pair assumption.

recommended_next_step: Do not spend the flagship revise budget on this angle; bank it as a field-tier finite witness and pivot to a non-definitional comparator-anchored frontier.
